_about.title: G3 directives — the opt-in directive seam + `repeat` + `watch` (completes the template engine; @agent-ui/components src/dom/)
_about.serves: goals.md G3 DoD2 (repeat) + DoD3 (watch + teardown isolation) + DoD4 (the G1–G3 html`` end-to-end proof) and plan.md §6 (directives = branded update fns threading state across commits, with dispose(); ship repeat + watch). Unblocks the A2UI renderer's dynamic list tail (LLD-C6), which REUSES `repeat`, and per-hole reactivity for controls. Companion to the shipped g3-templating.decomp.json (the engine core) — this is its directive follow-on.
_about.ground_state: The G3 template ENGINE is already built + green (template.ts, ~245 jsdom probes upstream): html/svg → inert TemplateResult; prepare(strings) → one <template> parse → a parts manifest, memoized in a WeakMap by `strings` IDENTITY; render(result, container) instantiate-or-update; ChildPart (text · nested template · array-by-position) + AttrPart/BooleanPart/PropertyPart/EventPart, each with its Object.is dirty-skip; classifyHoles position validation + the svg namespace. element.ts ships the render seam: `this.effect(() => { const r = this.render(); if (r instanceof TemplateResult) commitTemplate(r, this.renderRoot) })` — ONE connection-scope-owned render effect. NOT built (this decomp's whole scope): the directive seam, `repeat`, `watch`, and the scope_seam (the render-context threading watch needs). directives.ts does NOT exist; the d-simple riders (classMap/ref/unsafeHTML) are OUT of this brief's scope (a later slice) — the seam is designed not to preclude them.
_about.method: system-decompose, both planes, technical-architecture domain. OUTSIDE-IN = the directive subsystem divided by OWNING FILE into units (the seam hooks in template.ts · the ctx/scope_seam in template.ts+element.ts · repeat in directives.ts · watch in directives.ts · the dom barrel · the probe suites) — the structure. INSIDE-OUT = the directive CAPABILITIES (recognize+route a directive, thread state across commits, isolate teardown, thread the connection scope, keyed-reconcile, zero-move, reuse the part engine not the kernel, per-hole scoped waking, die/respawn on disconnect/reconnect, the e2e proof, surface, layering, no-regress, anti-vacuous) derived from goals.md G3 DoD2/3/4 + plan §6 INDEPENDENT of the structure so they can contradict it. `hosts` crosses them. The slice/owner_file/wave/build/dep/acceptance/gate keys overlay the file-disjoint fan-out plan; coverage_check ignores non-node/action/host keys.
_about.plane_independence: INSIDE-OUT was enumerated from the DoD/plan verbs (recognize a directive, thread state, isolate a throwing disposer, scope-own the per-hole effect, keyed-reconcile, zero-move, scoped-wake, die/respawn) BEFORE mapping to the file structure. The contradiction it surfaced and resolved: `cap-scope-seam` (the connection scope reaching the per-hole effect) is NOT owned by a single directive file — it is a host↔template SEAM realized in BOTH template.ts (ctx threading through render→createInstance→commitInstance→part) AND element.ts (the one-line ctx pass), and CONSUMED by watch. So it hosts on two structure nodes (u-ctx) plus its consumer (u-watch) rather than being force-fit into directives.ts. A second: `cap-anti-vacuous` (each new probe ships a negative control) is a cross-cutting verification need, not a feature — it hosts on the three probe nodes (u-test-seam/repeat/watch), not on a phantom 'NC' node.

_build_legend.now: buildable at the current ground state (the engine core is shipped)
_build_legend.prep: the serial PREP slice — pins the shared seam + the scope_seam BEFORE the directive fan-out
_build_legend.shared-edit: edits a file the integration slice owns (the dom barrel) — deferred to one serial slice
_build_legend.gate: verified by a script (npm run check && npm test) or a named probe; judgment where a reviewer confirms

_design._note: The LLD design embedded for the build slices. Names the contract + mechanism; the exact field/signature shapes are the implementer's, within these constraints. Every Decision below maps to an action (improvement #6) — see _decision_to_action.

seam.decision: A directive is a BRANDED factory → a stateful instance whose `update` threads state across commits of the SAME hole, with a `dispose()`. The ChildPart recognizes a branded DirectiveResult on commit and routes to the instance — the SOLE extension point; repeat/watch are riders, never special-cased in the part engine.
seam.shape: `directive(SomeDirectiveClass)` returns a factory `(...args) => DirectiveResult`. A DirectiveResult is inert: `{ [BRAND]: true, ctor: DirectiveClass, args }` (no DOM work at the call site, like TemplateResult). The seam base (BRAND symbol, DirectiveResult type, an `isDirectiveResult` guard, the `Directive` base contract `{ update(args, ctx?): unknown; dispose?(): void }`, a `NO_COMMIT` sentinel, the `RenderContext` type, the `directive()` factory) lives in template.ts (where ChildPart lives) so there is NO import cycle — repeat.ts/watch.ts/directives.ts import the seam FROM template.ts; template.ts imports nothing new.
seam.routing: ChildPart gains a `#directive: Directive | null`. On commit, BEFORE the text/template/array branch: if `isDirectiveResult(value)` — if `#directive` exists AND is the same ctor → call `#directive.update(args, ctx)`; else dispose the prior content/`#directive` and construct `new ctor(this)` (the part hands itself so a DOM-owning directive — repeat — can drive sub-parts), then `update(args, ctx)`. The instance's update either RETURNS a value the ChildPart commits through its normal child path (so a value-producing directive — watch's inner value — reuses text/template/array + Object.is), OR returns the `NO_COMMIT` sentinel when it manages its own DOM (repeat). Leaving directive mode (value is no longer a DirectiveResult) disposes `#directive`.
seam.teardown_isolation: Every `#directive?.dispose()` call site (replace, #clear, array sub-part drop) wraps the disposer in try/catch so a THROWING disposer is swallowed (reported, not rethrown) and sibling parts' teardown still runs. This is the load-bearing isolation invariant (rubric D3 = 5).

scope_seam.decision: RATIFIED (per the project record) — option (a): thread the host's render CONTEXT into the template render path explicitly. The per-hole watch effect MUST be owned by the connection scope (dies on disconnect, survives unrelated host re-renders), NOT created as a child of the transient render effect.
scope_seam.why_mandatory: Proven against the kernel: the host render effect is installed via `scope.run(() => createEffect(fn))`, so on its FIRST (synchronous) run activeOwner = the connection scope, but on every RE-run (scheduler flush()) EffectNode.run() executes with activeOwner = null (module default). A bare `effect()` created inside the render effect's body would therefore adopt activeOwner: scope-owned on first render, but UNOWNED (a leak) on every re-render. And the kernel does NOT auto-parent child effects to a running effect — only scopes dispose. So watch cannot rely on ambient ownership; it must be HANDED the scope-owned installer.
scope_seam.wiring: Define `interface RenderContext { effect(fn: () => void | (() => void)): () => void }` in template.ts. UIElement structurally satisfies it (it already has `effect(fn): () => void`). element.ts changes ONE line — the overridden-render commit becomes `commitTemplate(result, this.renderRoot, this)` (pass `this`). render() gains an optional trailing `ctx?: RenderContext` (the engine already documents this as a planned non-breaking addition); ctx threads through createInstance → commitInstance → each part.commit(value, ctx) → the directive's update(args, ctx). 2-arg render() calls keep working (ctx undefined). NOT re-exported as public surface — an internal host↔template seam, so D8 'surface is exactly the intended set' stays clean.
scope_seam.watch_uses_ctx: watch installs its per-hole effect via `ctx.effect(() => { const v = read(this.source); this.inner.commit(v) })`. ctx.effect = UIElement.effect = `scope.run(() => createEffect(fn))`, so the watch EffectNode is connection-scope-owned REGARDLESS of first-render vs re-render — exactly bypassing the activeOwner=null re-render trap. Reading the source INSIDE the effect subscribes the WATCH effect (activeConsumer = the watch effect during its run), so the host render effect gains NO source on the watched signal.
scope_seam.repeat_does_not_need_it: repeat reconciles synchronously DURING a commit driven by the host render effect (a new items array → host re-render → repeat diffs old vs new keys) or inside a watch hole (then the watch effect drives it). repeat owns NO persistent effect, so it ignores ctx and ships INDEPENDENT of the scope_seam — unblocking the program-critical d-repeat ahead of the watch decision.
scope_seam.element_ts_impact: additive, one line (the ctx pass) + render()'s optional param. No public surface change.

repeat.decision: `repeat(items, keyFn, template)` — a DOM-owning child directive (returns NO_COMMIT). It REUSES the existing ChildPart sub-part engine for per-item rendering (NOT a kernel/binding primitive — resolves the brief's open question: list reconciliation is pure DOM/key bookkeeping; it needs no signals/computed; the binding slice's per-path memo is a DATA-resolution concern, orthogonal). It owns no effect.
repeat.signature: `repeat<T>(items: Iterable<T>, keyFn: (item: T, index: number) => unknown, template: (item: T, index: number) => unknown)`. Each item renders into its OWN sub-ChildPart with its own anchor comment placed before the host part's anchor (the same mechanism as ChildPart's by-position array reconcile, but KEYED).
repeat.reconcile: Maintain an ordered list of { key, sub-part } + a key→sub-part map. On update: (1) build new keys; THROW on a duplicate key (a Set check — the dup-key guard). (2) Reconcile old↔new by key: head/tail two-pointer trim to leave unchanged prefix/suffix in place (zero moves), a key→old-index map for the middle; reuse a surviving key's sub-part (commit the new template value → Object.is/part skip), create a sub-part for a new key, dispose the sub-part for a removed key, and MOVE a node only when its key's order actually changed (move-by-identity: native parent.moveBefore where supported → element state/focus/selection preserved, else insertBefore fallback → identity preserved, focus dropped — the two-tier guarantee, ADR-0022). Append / remove / stable-prefix therefore perform ZERO DOM moves.
repeat.dispose: dispose() tears down every sub-part (each ChildPart.dispose removes its content + anchor). Sub-part disposers are isolated by the seam's try/catch.

watch.decision: `watch(source)` where source is `ReadonlySignal<T>` OR a thunk `() => T` — a value-producing child directive owning ONE inner sub-ChildPart and ONE connection-scope-owned effect. The effect reads the source under its OWN tracking context and commits the inner value through the inner sub-part (so the watched value can be text / a TemplateResult / an array / even a nested directive — full part machinery, including Object.is skip). The host render effect gains NO source on the watched signal.
watch.signature_recommendation: Accept BOTH a signal and a thunk: `watch(source: ReadonlySignal<T> | (() => T))`. The brief writes `watch(() => value, ...)`; the prior g3-templating decomp wrote `watch(signal)`. Recommend the union (a thunk is the general form; a bare signal is sugar) and an OPTIONAL mapper `watch(source, map?)` ≡ `watch(() => map(read(source)))`. FLAGGED as an open question for the host to ratify (see _open_questions).
watch.install_once: First update(args, ctx): store the source in a field the effect reads; install the effect via `ctx.effect(...)`; set `installed = true`. The effect returns a cleanup `() => { this.installed = false }`. On a subsequent host re-render (the SAME watch hole's update), set `this.source = newSource` and DO NOT re-run the effect (no per-render churn) — the effect's own tracked sources drive re-runs. Constraint (flagged): a thunk that SWAPS which signal it reads across host re-renders is not auto-re-tracked; wrap such logic in a computed or use a static (host-re-render) hole. This is the minimal leak-free no-churn semantics; recommend it over force-re-track-on-update.
watch.scoped_wake: A watched-signal change → markStale on the watch effect ONLY → schedule → flush → the watch effect re-runs → commits just the inner sub-part. The host render effect is never scheduled (it has no source on the signal). THE headline invariant (rubric D5).
watch.reconnect: On disconnect, the connection scope disposes → the watch effect disposes → its cleanup sets `installed = false` (zero residue; the watched signal has zero subscribers). The directive INSTANCE survives in the persisted ChildPart (element.ts clears neither renderRoot nor the RENDERED entry on disconnect). On reconnect, a NEW connection scope + a fresh render effect re-commit the same template → the watch hole's update(args, ctx) runs with the NEW ctx → sees `installed === false` → RE-installs under the new scope's ctx.effect. Dies on disconnect, respawns on reconnect, leak-free.

_decision_to_action._note: improvement #6 — every Decision in _design maps to a decomp action so nothing ships unhosted. No NEW ADR: this IMPLEMENTS the ratified scope_seam + the plan §6 directive contract (as the binding slice implemented LLD-C5 without a new ADR). Flag to host if an ADR is wanted for the RenderContext seam.

_first_slice: S0 PREP — the seam base (in template.ts) + the ChildPart directive-routing hook + dispose-isolation + the RenderContext/ctx threading (template.ts) + the one-line ctx pass (element.ts, the scope_seam). The smallest thing that makes a directive ROUTABLE and gives a per-hole effect a scope owner — every directive stands on it. No new public export yet (the seam is internal). Serial; repeat + watch fan out behind it.

_waves.wave-0-prep: S0 (serial, FIRST) — pins the shared seam + the scope_seam. OWNS template.ts (the seam base: BRAND/DirectiveResult/isDirectiveResult/Directive/NO_COMMIT/RenderContext/directive(); the ChildPart `#directive` routing + try/catch teardown isolation; ctx threaded through render→createInstance→commitInstance→part.commit) AND element.ts (the one-line `commitTemplate(result, this.renderRoot, this)` + render()'s optional ctx param). These two files are written ONCE here so the fan-out builds against a fixed seam. No other slice writes template.ts or element.ts.
_waves.wave-1: S1 `repeat` ∥ S2 `watch` — the directive fan-out, both depend ONLY on S0. FILE-DISJOINT if split into repeat.ts + watch.ts (each imports the seam from template.ts; no shared write) → buildable concurrently. If goals.md's single `directives.ts` is kept, S1+S2 collapse to ONE serial owner (repeat then watch in one file). Primary recommendation = SPLIT (repeat.ts + watch.ts) to realize the fan-out; the public surface is identical via the barrel (S3). FLAGGED — see _open_questions. S1 needs no scope_seam; S2 needs S0's ctx.
_waves.wave-2: S3 (serial, LAST) — the LONE integration/barrel edit: dom/index.ts re-exports { repeat, watch } (+ any intended-public seam types). The single shared file; done once. u-regress runs the standing gate (check + test + the import-layering trip-wire) and confirms the surface is exactly the intended set.
_waves.cross_workstream: This directives workstream is file-disjoint from the concurrent text-field workstream: directives touches template.ts/element.ts/repeat.ts/watch.ts/dom-index.ts; text-field touches controls/* and controls' own barrel. The only theoretical shared file is dom/index.ts — but controls export through controls/index.ts, not the dom barrel — so no collision. ASSUMPTION: the text-field workstream does NOT write dom/element.ts (it subclasses, not edits). Flag if false.

_test_nc_plan._note: Probes live beside the dom layer (src/dom/*.test.ts), named for the rubric dimension they pin (the probe id IS the evidence link). Each NEW gate ships a negative control anchored on a UNIQUE code token (best-practices); grep-confirm the mutation applied before trusting a green NC.
_test_nc_plan.directive-seam: rubric D3 — state threads across commits of the same hole (a stateful directive accumulates across re-renders); dispose() runs when the hole leaves directive mode.
_test_nc_plan.directive-teardown-isolation: rubric D3 (=5) — two sibling directives; the first's dispose() THROWS; replace both; assert the SECOND's dispose ran (a `siblingTorn` flag flips true). NC: remove the try/catch around the disposer → the throw aborts the loop → `siblingTorn` stays false → RED. Unique token: `siblingTorn` (appears only in the executable assertion).
_test_nc_plan.repeat-keyed: rubric D4 — reuse DOM by key across a data change (a surviving key's node identity is preserved).
_test_nc_plan.repeat-dup-throw: rubric D4 — a duplicate key throws. NC: remove the dup-key Set check → no throw → expect(...).toThrow goes RED. Unique token: the duplicate key literal `dupKey9`.
_test_nc_plan.repeat-move-identity: rubric D4 (=5) — capture node refs for keys [a,b,c]; reorder to [c,a,b]; assert each captured node `===` its key's node in the new order (MOVED by identity, NOT re-created). NC: replace move-by-identity with re-create-by-index → captured `===` fails → RED. IDENTITY is the always-true gate (every engine, incl. jsdom's insertBefore fallback); FOCUS/selection survival is the BROWSER-leg expectation where native moveBefore is supported, identity-only graceful fallback otherwise (ADR-0022) — `keepFocusC` is the stateful input whose identity is asserted here and whose focus the browser leg checks. Unique token: `keepFocusC` (the input's data attr, only in the assertion).
_test_nc_plan.repeat-zero-move: rubric D4 (=5) — spy on the parent's insertBefore (or instrument moves); appending one item / removing the tail / a stable prefix perform ZERO moves of existing nodes. NC: make the reconcile re-insert all nodes every update → move count > 0 → RED. Unique token: the appended item key `tailZ`.
_test_nc_plan.watch-isolated-rerender: rubric D5 (THE headline, mirrors the binding slice's per-path-waking) — a UIElement whose render() reads a host signal in a STATIC hole AND watch(watched) in a watch hole; a render-counter increments each render() run. Change `watched` → assert the watch hole updated AND the render-counter is UNCHANGED (the host effect did NOT re-run — it gained no source on `watched`). NC: make the host read `watched` directly (or create the watch effect WITHOUT ctx.effect, as a child of the render effect) → the render-counter increments → the 'unchanged' assertion goes RED. Unique token: the host render-count var `hostRenders` + the watched value `watchedOnly7`.
_test_nc_plan.watch-reconnect: rubric D5 (=5) — after disconnect, inspect(watchedSignal).subscribers === 0 (zero residue, leak-free, mirrors binding u-test-leak); reconnect → change `watched` → the hole updates again (respawn). NC: drop the effect cleanup's `installed=false` (or scope ownership) → after disconnect subscribers !== 0 OR the reconnected hole is stale → RED. Unique token: `respawnTick`.
_test_nc_plan.html-e2e: rubric D7 (the G1–G3 integration proof) — a real UIElement subclass: a tracked-prop change re-runs the host render effect and commits ONLY the changed hole (Object.is-skip end-to-end, asserted via a MutationObserver or a stable-node-identity check on the UNCHANGED hole), AND a watch-driven hole updates WITHOUT the host re-rendering. Not a unit stub — through connect/disconnect.
_test_nc_plan.no-regress + surface + layering: the standing suite (npm run check && npm test) stays green; the import-layering trip-wire stays green (template.ts imports nothing new; repeat.ts/watch.ts import only ../reactive + the seam from template.ts); the dom barrel exports exactly { repeat, watch } added (no internal BRAND/Directive/part field leaks); the directive/part discriminated unions are exhaustiveness-checked (never default).

_predicted_gates.decomp: python3 .claude/skills/system-decompose/scripts/coverage_check.py .claude/docs/decompositions/g3-directives.decomp.json --strict  ->  exit 0  (== coverage: 16 nodes · 14 actions · 29 hosts ==)
_predicted_gates.standing: npm run check && npm test  ->  green (S0 keeps the ~245-probe engine suite green — no behavior change; S1/S2 add repeat/watch probes; S3 keeps the surface test green)
_predicted_gates.rubric: .claude/docs/rubrics/template.md D3 ≥ 4 (seam) · D4 ≥ 4 (repeat) · D5 ≥ 4 (watch) · D7 ≥ 4 (e2e) · D8 ≥ 4 (surface+layering) — the gates these slices must clear.

_open_questions: watch signature: the brief writes `watch(() => value, ...)`; the prior g3-templating decomp wrote `watch(signal)`. RECOMMEND the union `watch(source: ReadonlySignal<T> | (() => T))` + an optional mapper `watch(source, map?)`. Host to ratify the exact shape (esp. whether the mapper ships in G3 or defers).

file layout for the fan-out: goals.md names a single `directives.ts` (repeat, watch, classMap, ref, unsafeHTML). To make repeat ∥ watch FILE-DISJOINT (the brief's fan-out), split into repeat.ts + watch.ts (seam stays in template.ts). RECOMMEND the split (public surface identical via the dom barrel); if goals.md's single-file layout is binding, S1+S2 collapse to one serial directives.ts slice (no intra-workstream parallelism). Host's call.

watch source re-track: on a host re-render that SWAPS the watched source thunk to read a different signal, the recommended semantics do NOT auto-re-track (no per-render churn) — a thunk must read stable signals (wrap dynamic selection in a computed). Confirm this constraint is acceptable, or accept per-render re-track churn.

RenderContext ADR: threading RenderContext into render() is an internal host↔template seam implementing the RATIFIED scope_seam — no new ADR proposed (mirrors the binding slice implementing LLD-C5). Flag if an ADR record is wanted for the render-context shape.

nodes.label: G3 directives — the directive seam + repeat + watch (+ the scope_seam)

the directive seam in template.ts — BRAND/DirectiveResult/isDirectiveResult/Directive base/NO_COMMIT/directive() factory + the ChildPart `#directive` routing (recognize a branded result → instantiate-or-update; leave directive mode → dispose) + try/catch teardown isolation (a throwing disposer doesn't abort siblings)
nodes.acceptance: rubric D3: a branded factory threads state across commits of the SAME hole; the SOLE extension point (repeat/watch are riders, not special-cased); a throwing disposer is isolated. No public re-export yet (internal seam).

nodes.label: the scope_seam — RenderContext (template.ts) threaded through render→createInstance→commitInstance→part.commit→directive.update(args, ctx); element.ts passes `this` (one line) so a per-hole directive effect is owned by the CONNECTION SCOPE (via ctx.effect), not the transient render effect
nodes.acceptance: render() gains an optional trailing ctx; 2-arg calls unbroken; element.ts changes ONE line (commitTemplate(r, renderRoot, this)); ctx NOT re-exported (internal). Proven necessary against the kernel: a re-render flushes with activeOwner=null, so a bare effect() would leak — ctx.effect re-establishes scope ownership explicitly.

nodes.label: repeat (the keyed reconcile — A2UI LLD-C6 reuses it)

repeat(items, keyFn, template) in repeat.ts — a DOM-owning child directive (returns NO_COMMIT) reconciling a keyed list via per-item ChildPart sub-parts: reuse-by-key, move-by-identity, dup-key throw; append/remove/stable-prefix cost zero DOM moves; owns no effect (needs no scope_seam)
nodes.programFlag: DELIVERS `repeat` — the A2UI renderer's dynamic list tail (LLD-C6) reuses it; its scheduling (move-by-identity, zero-move stable prefix) is a program-level interface.
nodes.acceptance: rubric D4: head/tail trim + key→old-index map for the middle; throw on a duplicate key; a reorder MOVES nodes by identity (identity preserved always; element state/focus preserved where native moveBefore is supported, identity-only fallback otherwise — ADR-0022); append/remove/stable-prefix → zero insertBefore on existing nodes. REUSES the ChildPart engine, NOT a kernel/binding primitive (resolves the brief's reuse question).
nodes.gate: npm test (repeat-keyed · repeat-dup-throw · repeat-move-identity · repeat-zero-move + NCs)

nodes.label: watch(source) in watch.ts — a value-producing child directive owning one inner sub-ChildPart + ONE connection-scope-owned effect (via ctx.effect) that reads the source under its own tracking context and commits the inner value through the inner part; the host effect gains no source on the watched signal; installed-flag respawn on reconnect via the effect cleanup
nodes.seamFlag: REQUIRES the scope_seam (u-ctx) — the per-hole effect must be connection-scope-owned, not a child of the transient render effect.
nodes.acceptance: rubric D5: a watched-signal change updates ONLY the hole WITHOUT re-running the host render effect; the effect is scope-owned (survives unrelated host re-renders, dies on disconnect with zero residue, respawns on reconnect). Inner value flows through the part (text/template/array/nested directive + Object.is skip).

nodes.label: dom/index.ts — re-export exactly { repeat, watch } (+ any intended-public seam types); keep the seam internals (BRAND, DirectiveResult, the part classes, NO_COMMIT, RenderContext) unexported; inward-only layering preserved
nodes.acceptance: rubric D8: the surface is exactly the intended set with no internal field leak; the one file two slices could touch — done once, serial, last (grows incrementally as S1/S2 land if desired, locks here).

nodes.label: verification (the probe suites + the standing gate)
seam probes — directive-seam (state threads across commits; dispose runs) + directive-teardown-isolation (a throwing disposer leaves siblings torn down) + its negative control on `siblingTorn`
nodes.acceptance: rubric D3 green; NC: removing the try/catch turns the `siblingTorn` assertion RED (unique token, grep-confirmed).

nodes.label: repeat probes — repeat-keyed, repeat-dup-throw (NC on `dupKey9`), repeat-move-identity (NC on `keepFocusC`), repeat-zero-move (NC on `tailZ`)
nodes.acceptance: rubric D4 = 5: reuse-by-key, dup-throw, reorder MOVES by identity (identity always; focus/selection where native moveBefore supported, identity-only fallback — ADR-0022), append/remove/stable-prefix zero moves; each NC anchored on a unique token, grep-confirmed.

nodes.label: watch probes — watch-isolated-rerender (the host render-counter `hostRenders` does NOT increment on a watched-signal change; NC routes the watch through the host → RED) + watch-reconnect (zero subscribers after disconnect; respawn after reconnect; NC on the dropped cleanup)
nodes.acceptance: rubric D5 = 5: the headline scoped-wake probe with a per-path-waking-style NC (mirrors the binding slice); leak-free + respawn pinned; NCs on `hostRenders`/`watchedOnly7`/`respawnTick`, grep-confirmed.

nodes.label: the G1–G3 html`` end-to-end proof — a real UIElement subclass: a tracked-prop change commits ONLY the changed hole (Object.is-skip end-to-end), and a watch hole updates without the host re-rendering; through connect/disconnect
nodes.acceptance: rubric D7 = 5: not a unit stub — render() composes with the host scope-owned effect; the unchanged hole's node identity is preserved on a tracked-prop change; a watch hole updates with the host render-counter unchanged.

nodes.label: no-regression + surface + layering gate — the standing suite + tsc stay green after the seam/ctx wiring; the import-layering trip-wire stays green; the dom surface is exactly the intended set
nodes.acceptance: npm run check && npm test green; the shipped ~245-probe engine behavior unchanged; template.ts imports nothing new; repeat.ts/watch.ts import only ../reactive + the template.ts seam; no enum/namespace/decorator; the unions are exhaustiveness-checked.

actions.label: a directive is a branded factory whose update threads state across commits of the SAME hole, with dispose(); the child part recognizes a directive result and routes commits to it — the SOLE extension point
directive teardown is isolated — a throwing disposer does not abort sibling parts' teardown
the host's connection scope is threaded into the template render path (RenderContext.effect) so a per-hole directive effect is owned by the connection scope, not the transient render effect
repeat reconciles a keyed list — reuse DOM by key, move by identity, throw on a duplicate key
append / remove / a stable prefix cost zero DOM moves in repeat
repeat drives per-item ChildPart sub-parts (reuses the part engine, NOT a kernel/binding primitive) and owns no persistent effect (so it needs no scope_seam)
watch(source) updates ONLY its hole on the source's change WITHOUT re-running the host render effect (the host gains no source on the watched signal)
the per-hole watch effect is owned by the connection scope (created via RenderContext.effect), so it survives unrelated host re-renders
the watch subscription dies on disconnect (zero residue) and respawns on reconnect

one UIElement renders html`` end-to-end — a tracked-prop change re-runs the host render effect committing ONLY the changed hole, and a watch hole updates without the host re-rendering (the G1–G3 proof)
the dom barrel exports exactly repeat + watch (+ intended-public seam types) — no internal part/manifest/directive-base field leaks
template.ts/repeat.ts/watch.ts keep inward-only layering (import only ../reactive / the template.ts seam); no enum/namespace/decorator; the part + directive unions are exhaustiveness-checked
the shipped template engine behavior + the standing suite stay green after the seam/ctx wiring
each new probe (teardown-isolation, repeat-move-identity/zero-move/dup-throw, watch-isolated-rerender/reconnect) ships a negative control on a UNIQUE code token proving it can go red
